title: seseli montanum subsp. polyphyllum
intro: Seseli montanum subsp. polyphyllum, also known as polyphyllous mountain chervil or mountain chervil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carrot family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating respiratory issues and soothing irritated tissues. The health benefits of Seseli montanum subsp. polyphyllum include relief from bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Seseli montanum subsp. polyphyllum can be found in various forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional folk medicine to address respiratory and inflammatory issues.
